title: UV Crosslinking of Suspension Cells for eCLIP
author: Eric Van Nostrand
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.z62f9ge
materials: Gently resuspend until the sample is homogeneous
Pool all cells per biosample (if multiple plates)

steps.title: Aliquot at most 60×10 6 cells...
steps.instruction: Aliquot at most 60×10 6 cells (re-suspended in 1x DPBS) in at least 3 mL total volume to a standard 10cm tissue culture grade plate. Note : Ensure the cells are evenly dispersed and the plate is fully covered (3 mL should be sufficient volume).

steps.title: Place the above (plate plus ice...
steps.instruction: Place the above (plate plus ice or cooling block) into the UV cross-linker. Note: Ensure the plate is leveled. Remove tissue culture plate lid for cross-linking.

steps.title: After crosslinking is completed, transfer the...
steps.instruction: After crosslinking is completed, transfer the cells to a 50 mL conical tube

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet(s) in 25 mL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet(s) in 25 mL of 1x DPBS at room temperature.

steps.title: Spin down remaining sample in 50ml...
steps.instruction: Spin down remaining sample in 50ml conical tube(s) at 200g for 5 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Count cell concentration (either with automated cell counter or hemocytometer)
steps.instruction: Count cell concentration (either with automated cell counter or hemocytometer)

steps.title: Wash plate once with 7mL of...
steps.instruction: Wash plate once with 7mL of 1x DPBS and add to the same 50 mL tube.

steps.title: Cross-link at 254-nm UV with an...
steps.instruction: Cross-link at 254-nm UV with an energy setting of 400 mJoules/cm 2 Note: this is a setting of 4000 on many cross-linkers which display values in 0.1 mJoules/cm 2

steps.title: Count cell concentration (either with automated...
steps.instruction: Count cell concentration (either with automated cell counter or hemocytometer) Note : ensure cells are re-suspended well before counting

steps.title: Resuspend in the desired amount for...
steps.instruction: Resuspend in the desired amount for flash freezing Typically 20×10 6 cells per mL.

steps.title: Centrifuge the 50 ml conical tube...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the 50 ml conical tube at 200g for five minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: After frozen (at least thirty seconds),...
steps.instruction: After frozen (at least thirty seconds), remove from the liquid nitrogen and store at -80°C.

steps.title: Aspirate the supernatant and freeze by...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the supernatant and freeze by submerging the epi-tubes completely in liquid nitrogen.

steps.title: Spin down at 200g for five minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Spin down at 200g for five minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Transfer desired amount into 1
steps.instruction: Transfer desired amount into 1.5 mL Eppendorf Safe-Lock Tubes (or equivalent) Typically 1mL of 20×10 6 cells per mL.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 200g for 5 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 200g for 5 minutes at room temperature

steps.title: Transfer cells with media to 50mL conical tube(s)
steps.instruction: Transfer cells with media to 50mL conical tube(s)

steps.title: Resuspend cells to no more than...
steps.instruction: Resuspend cells to no more than 20×10 6 cells per mL

steps.title: Place the tissue culture plate on...
steps.instruction: Place the tissue culture plate on leveled ice or a cooling block pre-chilled to 4°C
